description: Fields: definition, algebraically closed fields, field extensions, finite fields, characteristic of a field
Vector spaces over a field: definition, dimension, subspaces, quotient spaces, direct sums, bases
Linear maps and endomorphisms: kernel, image, isomorphism theorems, linear operators on finite-dimensional spaces
Matrix algebra: matrix multiplication, trace, determinant, similarity, matrix units
Eigenvalues and eigenvectors: characteristic polynomial, eigenspaces, diagonalization
Bilinear forms and inner products: Hermitian inner product on complex vector spaces, orthogonality
Groups: definition, subgroups, normal subgroups, quotient groups, group homomorphisms, group actions, conjugacy classes, center of a group
Symmetric group: permutations, cycle decomposition, conjugacy classes of S_n, sign homomorphism
Finite abelian groups: classification by elementary divisors, structure theorem, characters of abelian groups
Group algebra k[G]: construction, basis indexed by group elements, convolution product
Tensor product of vector spaces: construction, universal property, tensor product of linear maps
Rings and ideals: definition of rings, two-sided ideals, quotient rings, nilpotent ideals, Jacobson radical
Modules over rings: left modules, right modules, submodules, quotient modules, free modules, simple modules
Dual vector space V* and natural pairing, dual maps (transpose/adjoint of linear maps)
Polynomial rings k[x]: division algorithm, irreducible polynomials, minimal polynomial of a linear operator
Algebraic integers and algebraic number theory basics: algebraic integers form a ring, norm of algebraic integers
Combinatorics of partitions and Young diagrams: partitions of integers, Young tableaux, hook lengths, content of a cell
Lie algebras: Jacobi identity, bracket operation, examples (gl_n, sl_n, so_n, sp_2n)
Universal enveloping algebra U(g) of a Lie algebra: construction and basic properties
Topological groups and Lie groups: continuous group homomorphisms, examples (GL_n(R), SO(n), U(n))
Complex analysis basics: roots of unity, absolute values of complex numbers, triangle inequality for sums of roots of unity
Symmetric polynomials and power sums: elementary symmetric polynomials, power sum symmetric polynomials, Newton's identities
Finite fields: existence and uniqueness of F_q, multiplicative group, quadratic residues, field extensions of finite fields
Semidirect products of groups: construction and characterization
Graph theory basics: undirected graphs, connected components, trees, adjacency matrix, positive definite quadratic forms
Category theory basics assumed in Chapter 7: sets, functions, composition, associativity (for motivating examples)
Exact sequences of modules: short exact sequences, left/right exactness of functors, splitting of exact sequences
Hom functor and its properties: Hom_A(M,N) as a vector space, left exactness, contravariance in first argument
Zorn's lemma: every partially ordered set in which every chain has an upper bound has a maximal element
Jordan-Hölder theorem: any two composition series of a finite-length module have the same length and the same composition factors (up to reordering and isomorphism)
Wedderburn-Artin theorem: a semisimple artinian ring is isomorphic to a finite direct product of matrix rings over division rings
Dynkin diagram classification: the complete list of positive definite Cartan matrices / Dynkin diagrams (A_n, D_n, E_6, E_7, E_8)
Gabriel's theorem: a connected quiver has finitely many indecomposable representations if and only if its underlying graph is a Dynkin diagram of type ADE
Classification of irreducible representations of sl(2): the (n+1)-dimensional representations V_n for n >= 0 exhaust all irreducibles
Vandermonde determinant formula: det(x_i^{N-j}) = product_{i<j}(x_i - x_j)
Algebraic conjugates of roots of unity are roots of unity; product of algebraic conjugates of an algebraic integer is a rational integer
Every module over a ring is a quotient of a free module (existence of free resolutions)
Characters of representations are class functions; character of a direct sum is sum of characters; character of a tensor product is product of characters
Nilpotent ideals and nilpotency: a nilpotent ideal I satisfies I^n = 0 for some n; properties of nilpotent elements in algebras
Dimension counting arguments: if V = W ⊕ W' as vector spaces then dim V = dim W + dim W'; rank-nullity theorem
Properties of the trace: tr(AB) = tr(BA), trace of identity is dimension, trace is basis-independent
Idempotents in algebras: decomposition of identity into orthogonal idempotents, primitive idempotents, lifting of idempotents
Krull-Schmidt theorem: every module of finite length has a unique decomposition into indecomposable direct summands (up to isomorphism and reordering)
Schur-Weyl duality: the commuting actions of GL(V) and S_n on V^{⊗n} give a double centralizer relationship
Frobenius reciprocity: Hom_G(Ind_H^G V, W) ≅ Hom_H(V, Res_H W) naturally
Mackey's theorem (intertwining number formula for induced representations and double coset decomposition)
Generalized Schur orthogonality relations: orthogonality of matrix coefficients of irreducible representations over compact or finite groups
Averaging (Reynolds) operator for finite group actions: (1/|G|) Σ_g ρ(g) is the projection onto invariants when char k does not divide |G|
PBW (Poincaré-Birkhoff-Witt) theorem: the universal enveloping algebra U(g) has an ordered monomial basis
Nakayama's lemma: if M is a finitely generated module over a local ring and IM = M for I in the maximal ideal, then M = 0
Ext functors: definition as derived functors of Hom, long exact sequence in Ext, Ext^1 classifies extensions
Deformation theory of algebras: Hochschild cohomology, formal deformations, Maurer-Cartan equation
Hochschild cohomology of an algebra: definition via bar resolution, interpretation of low-degree cohomology groups
Quadratic forms: positive definiteness, Sylvester's criterion, relationship between quadratic forms and symmetric bilinear forms
Abelian categories: kernels, cokernels, exact sequences in abstract categorical setting
Natural transformations and functor categories: Yoneda lemma and representable functors
Adjoint functors: unit and counit of adjunction, adjoint functor theorem (existence statements)
Tensor categories and monoidal structure: associativity and unit constraints, coherence, braiding
